requirements: Use exact SERP evidence when available.
Add Yoast title, description, focus keyphrase.
Optimize for Google and AI answer engines.
Define the audience, mission fit, and unique angle before drafting.
Classify search intent as informational, navigational, commercial, or transactional.
Match the article format and CTA to the dominant search intent.
Use an inverted-pyramid opening: answer the main question immediately, then expand.
Plan a clear heading structure before drafting and check it again during repair.
Keep paragraphs short, scannable, and led by clear topic sentences.
Add internal links so visitors can navigate related posts and pages.
Add Article schema and relevant FAQ/Breadcrumb/Organization schema opportunities where appropriate.
Use AI as a drafting and structuring aid, then run human-quality and factual repair before publishing.

gsc.diagnosis: Use a GSC property your OAuth user owns, e.g. sc-domain:meetlyra.app, or add dylanangloher@gmail.com to the exact Search Console property.

ga4.error: 403 User does not have sufficient permissions for this property. To learn more about Property ID, see https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/reporting/data/v1/property-id.
ga4.diagnosis: Add dylanangloher@gmail.com to the GA4 property access management with Viewer or Analyst access, and confirm GA4_PROPERTY_ID is the numeric property ID.

recommendations: Keep Yoast's native schema graph enabled; extend it through Yoast filters rather than adding duplicate standalone JSON-LD when possible.
Use Article schema for every generated post and reference Organization/WebSite graph pieces already emitted by Yoast.
Use Breadcrumb schema on posts so the graph connects blog posts back to the blog and site root.
Only use FAQ schema when the article contains a genuine FAQ block with visible questions and answers.
Build topic-cluster schema notes from internal-link groups once the blog has 5+ real posts.

next_actions: Prioritize `AI marketing agent for SEO content` as the next content opportunity unless GSC quick wins outrank it.
Add dylanangloher@gmail.com to the GA4 property access management with Viewer or Analyst access, and confirm GA4_PROPERTY_ID is the numeric property ID.
Use a GSC property your OAuth user owns, e.g. sc-domain:meetlyra.app, or add dylanangloher@gmail.com to the exact Search Console property.
Use the internal link candidates in the next writer run to strengthen topical authority.
